title: SEBI Fork
intro: It is 2020. Multi-cap funds were sold as go-anywhere products but many settled into large caps. You are shaping the rulebook response.

nodes.text: The complaint: multi-caps are not true to label. Investors thought they bought diversification across market caps; portfolios disagree. What do you do?

choices.label: Force every multi-cap to hold ≥25% in large, mid, and small
choices.note: You chose hard floors for multi-cap.

choices.label: Ignore it — labels are marketing, not promises
choices.note: You left the label-substance gap unfixed.

nodes.text: Trust erodes. Savers stop believing category names. The industry still faces the same drift complaint a year later. Try again with a structural fix.
Floors make multi-caps truer to the name. Managers object: some books cannot rebalance overnight; others want a freer sleeve for clients. Industry lobbies for an escape hatch. Do you offer one?

choices.label: Hold the line — no new category; multi-cap means floors
Create flexi-cap: ≥65% equity, no mid/small floors

nodes.text: Every diversified ‘go anywhere’ product must live with floors. That honesty helps labelling — but many managers still want a freedom product for investors who accept active large-cap bias.

choices.label: Acknowledge the demand and create flexi-cap anyway

nodes.text: Flexi-cap exists. Years later, many flexi-caps again sit in large caps with little mid/small motion. What lesson sticks?

choices.label: Rules can rename buckets; they cannot force conviction to use freedom
Flexi-caps always beat because freedom = alpha

ending.principle: You can rename a category and write floors — you cannot force managers to use freedom.
ending.debrief: Flexi-cap was the escape hatch after multi-cap floors. Style drift can reappear inside any label that allows it. Read holdings; don’t worship the category name.

ending.principle: Freedom is an opportunity set, not a performance guarantee.
ending.debrief: If flexi-caps hug large caps, you may not get the mid/small participation the freedom story implies. Check behaviour, not just the mandate.
